---
title: Protein Data Bank
alias: &ALIAS proteindb
href: https://www.rcsb.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The RCSB Protein Data Bank (PDB) is the global repository for 3D structural data of biological macromolecules (proteins, DNA, RNA), providing free access to data and tools for visualization and analysis.

## Key Features

- Archives over 200,000 experimentally determined 3D structures from X-ray, NMR, and cryo-EM
- Provides structure validation reports, sequence-to-structure mappings, and ligand binding data
- Supports 3D structure visualization with NGL Viewer and downloadable PDB/mmCIF/FASTA files
- Enables structure-based drug discovery with binding site analysis and ligand interaction tools
- Integrates with UniProt, PubMed, and chemical databases for cross-linked functional context

## Use Cases in Research

- Accessing 3D structures of AMR proteins (beta-lactamases, ribosomes) for structure-based drug design
- Performing molecular docking studies targeting pathogen-specific structural features
- Training structure-prediction and protein-language AI models using PDB as ground truth
- Analyzing binding modes of antibiotic compounds within experimentally resolved drug targets
- Comparing structural differences between wildtype and mutant resistance proteins

## Getting Started

Visit [Protein Data Bank](https://www.rcsb.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
